sources.notes: Count completions of a partial permutation that can be a riffle shuffle.
Official editorial fixes a split k and counts how many missing positions can be colored left/right using binomial coefficients, in O(n) per split.

annotation.summary: Given a partial permutation with -1 entries, count completions that are a riffle shuffle of the identity permutation.
annotation.constraints: Easy version allows O(n^2) by iterating every split and scanning the array.
annotation.core_idea: For a valid completed permutation that is not sorted, the split k is determined by the first inversion k appearing after k+1. Fix k and color values <=k as left and values >k as right. While scanning the partial permutation, every known left/right value imposes how many missing positions before it must be filled by that side; choose those positions with precomputed binomial coefficients. Sum over all splits and subtract overcounts of the sorted permutation.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) per test case after factorial precomputation.

solution_variants.name: split-enumeration-combinatorics
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate k, scan the partial permutation, and multiply combinations for each gap of unknown entries.

tags.evidence: Each fixed split is counted by multiplying binomial choices for unknown positions in gaps.
The object counted is completions of a partial permutation satisfying a shuffle property.
The easy version directly iterates all possible split positions.
